rationale: Scope/leaf lens: 3 of 4 invariants hold cleanly, the 4th is a benign, precedented inaccuracy. (1) Config-free leaf HOLDS: `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep internal/config` is empty; internal/panel source imports no config/git/exec (only stdlib); `Create(dir, CreateInput)` takes all plain values (strings/int/*string), never a config or executor type. (2) PanelGateDecision UNCHANGED: `git diff 2f894401..2da52b09 -- internal/panel/gate.go` is byte-empty; `PanelGateDecision(GateFacts) Decision` and `ResolveGateFacts` signatures identical. (3) spec-approve emits NO plan coverage: validate/spec.go raises only `impacted-domains-resolve` + `adr-touchpoint-missing`; explicit comments keep `adr-coverage-*`/`adr-cite-irrelevant` at plan-approve (R7c holds). (4) Scope: the only out-of-workflow source edit is internal/redact/redact.go (core-owned), a 3-line additive allowlist edit registering panel/verify/tally verbs — necessary, matching spec 101's `release`-token precedent, and tolerated by the divergence gate (fbel.4 merged via `mindspec complete`). The spec's 'sole impacted domain / no source outside workflow' prose slightly over-claims, but no load-bearing invariant is harmed and impl-approve is not at risk.

findings.summary: Config-free leaf verified: go list -deps ./internal/panel has no internal/config; Create imports only stdlib and takes plain values (CreateInput = strings/int/*string). R7b satisfied.
PanelGateDecision inputs/outputs unchanged — internal/panel/gate.go diff over 2f894401..2da52b09 is empty. R7a satisfied.
validate/spec.go spec-approve parity emits only impacted-domains-resolve and adr-touchpoint-missing; no adr-coverage-missing / adr-cite-irrelevant leaks to spec-approve. R7c satisfied.

internal/redact/redact.go (core-owned, NOT under any workflow OWNERSHIP glob) was edited in fbel.4 to add panel/verify/tally to CommandTokens/SubcommandTokens. This contradicts the spec's repeated claim that workflow is the SOLE impacted domain and 'no path outside the workflow OWNERSHIP globs is edited as source' / core has 'no source... changes'. The edit itself is correct and necessary (a new CLI verb must join the redaction allowlist or its name is scrubbed from traces) and matches the established pattern (spec 101's `release` token in the same maps). The divergence gate tolerated it (the bead merged through `mindspec complete`), so it will not block impl-approve. This is a spec-prose accuracy note, not a code defect — no rewind needed under forward-only lifecycle. Recommend noting in the impl-approve handoff that spec 110 also touched core's redact allowlist as a routine verb-registration companion edit.
